compiled_state.title: Implement scan-docs.sh file-type guard (binary detection, size guard, path validation, skip logging)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Context
Implements the file-type guard portion of scan-docs.sh. This is the first atomic unit — it can be committed independently because a scan-docs.sh that skips binary/large files and validates paths is a fully functional (if limited) script. Text extraction is added in T4.

## What to Build
Create `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh` with:

### Interface
```
scan-docs.sh <doc_folder>
```
- Validates `<doc_folder>`: resolves to an accessible directory, rejects paths containing `..` or symlinks pointing outside the resolved root
- Exits 1 with `ERROR:path_traversal` on stderr if path traversal detected
- Exits 1 with `ERROR:not_found` on stderr if directory does not exist or is not readable
- Scans files in `<doc_folder>` (non-recursive at this stage; Phase 0.5 in SKILL.md controls recursion)
- For each file:
  - Skip binary files: use `file --mime-type` to detect; skip if mime starts with `application/` (non-text) or `image/` or `audio/` or `video/`; log `SKIP:binary <filename>` to stderr
  - Skip files >200KB: `stat -c%s` (Linux) or `stat -f%z` (macOS); log `SKIP:size <filename>` to stderr
  - Skip files with non-text encoding (read first 512 bytes; if contains null byte, treat as binary)
- On success, outputs JSON to stdout: `{"facts":[],"skipped":["<reason>:<filename>",...]}` (empty facts array — extraction added in T4)
- Exits 0 on success (even if all files were skipped)
- Cap: process at most 50 files per run (log WARNING:file_cap_reached to stderr if more exist)

### Implementation notes
- Use `_PLUGIN_ROOT` resolved via BASH_SOURCE for any internal references
- Use `command -v file` guard before using `file` command; fallback to null-byte check if unavailable
- No new runtime dependencies: stdlib bash, `file`, `stat` (all standard)
- `chmod +x` the script after creation

## TDD Requirement
Tests in `tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh` (created in T1) must pass after this task. Run: `bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh` exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh
- [ ] Binary file is skipped with SKIP:binary on stderr
  Verify: printf '\x00\x01\x02' > /tmp/test_bin.bin && DIR=$(mktemp -d) && cp /tmp/test_bin.bin $DIR/ && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR 2>&1 | grep -q 'SKIP:binary'
- [ ] Large file (>200KB) is skipped with SKIP:size on stderr
  Verify: DIR=$(mktemp -d) && dd if=/dev/urandom bs=201k count=1 2>/dev/null | base64 > $DIR/large.txt && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR 2>&1 | grep -q 'SKIP:size'
- [ ] Path traversal rejected with exit 1
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh "../etc"; [ $? -eq 1 ]
- [ ] Nonexistent folder rejected with exit 1
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh /nonexistent/path/xyz; [ $? -eq 1 ]
- [ ] Empty folder returns exit 0 with {"facts":[],"skipped":[]} on stdout
  Verify: DIR=$(mktemp -d) && OUT=$(bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR) && python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.loads('$OUT'); assert d['facts']==[] and d['skipped']==[]"
- [ ] test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh
